name: storage
context: Cloud Storage for Firebase — upload, download, and manage files with security rules, resumable uploads, metadata control, and CDN delivery
info: Cloud Storage for Firebase provides scalable file storage backed by Google Cloud Storage buckets. Storage references (ref) point to files using paths like 'images/photo.jpg'. Upload files with uploadBytes for small files, uploadBytesResumable for large files with progress tracking and pause/resume, or uploadString for base64/data-URL/raw strings. Download files with getDownloadURL to get a long-lived HTTPS URL with token, getBytes to fetch into memory, or getBlob for browser blob objects. Manage file metadata (contentType, cacheControl, customMetadata) with getMetadata and updateMetadata. Delete files with deleteObject. List files and prefixes in a directory with listAll or paginate with list. Security rules in storage.rules control per-path read/write access using request.auth, resource.size, resource.contentType, and custom claims. CORS configuration is required for browser downloads from custom domains. Multiple storage buckets are supported — pass a gs:// URL to getStorage. The Admin SDK (firebase-admin) bypasses security rules and accesses buckets directly via admin.storage().bucket() with upload, download, getSignedUrl, and delete methods. Cloud Functions can trigger on storage object finalize, delete, archive, and metadata update events for server-side processing like thumbnail generation or virus scanning.

sections.title: Storage references
sections.body: Storage references are pointers to files or directories in a Cloud Storage bucket. Create with `ref(storage, 'path/to/file')`. References expose `fullPath`, `name`, `bucket`, `parent`, and `root` properties. Navigate with child refs via `ref(parentRef, 'child')`. References do not create files — they are used as targets for upload, download, delete, and metadata operations.

sections.title: Upload operations
sections.body: Three upload methods: `uploadBytes(ref, data)` for small files from File, Blob, or Uint8Array; `uploadBytesResumable(ref, data)` for large files with progress callbacks, pause/resume/cancel support, and error handling; `uploadString(ref, string, format)` for raw, base64, base64url, or data_url strings. All accept optional UploadMetadata with contentType, cacheControl, and customMetadata. Resumable uploads fire 'state_changed' events with bytesTransferred and totalBytes.

sections.title: Download operations
sections.body: `getDownloadURL(ref)` returns a long-lived HTTPS URL with an access token — the token persists until revoked. `getBytes(ref, maxBytes?)` downloads file content into an ArrayBuffer (default 256MB max). `getBlob(ref, maxBytes?)` downloads as a browser Blob. For server-side downloads, use the Admin SDK `bucket.file(path).download()`. Download URLs from getDownloadURL include the token in the query string and work without authentication.

sections.title: File metadata
sections.body: `getMetadata(ref)` returns a FullMetadata object with contentType, size, timeCreated, updated, md5Hash, and customMetadata. `updateMetadata(ref, metadata)` updates writable properties: contentType, contentDisposition, contentEncoding, contentLanguage, cacheControl, and customMetadata. Set a metadata property to null to delete it. Custom metadata is a flat string-to-string map — no nested objects.

sections.title: Security rules
sections.body: Storage security rules live in `storage.rules` and use `match` patterns on file paths. Available variables: `request.auth` (user identity and custom claims), `request.resource` (incoming file metadata for writes), `resource` (existing file metadata for reads). Common constraints: `request.resource.size < 5 * 1024 * 1024` for max file size, `request.resource.contentType.matches('image/.*')` for type restriction, `request.auth.uid == userId` for user-scoped paths. Deploy rules with `firebase deploy --only storage`.

sections.title: List and delete files
sections.body: `listAll(ref)` returns all items (file refs) and prefixes (folder refs) under a path. `list(ref, { maxResults, pageToken })` paginates results. `deleteObject(ref)` deletes a single file. There is no bulk delete in the client SDK — loop over `listAll` results and delete individually. The Admin SDK supports `bucket.deleteFiles({ prefix })` for bulk operations.

sections.title: Admin SDK access
sections.body: The Admin SDK (`firebase-admin/storage`) accesses the underlying Google Cloud Storage bucket directly, bypassing all security rules. Use `getStorage().bucket()` to get a Bucket instance. Methods: `bucket.upload(localPath, options)`, `bucket.file(path).download(options)`, `bucket.file(path).getSignedUrl(options)`, `bucket.file(path).delete()`, `bucket.getFiles({ prefix })`. Signed URLs from the Admin SDK require a service account and have configurable expiry — different from client SDK download URLs which use Firebase tokens.

links.context: Firebase ecosystem — project setup, configuration, client and admin SDK initialization
Authentication — request.auth in security rules depends on signed-in users
Cloud Functions — trigger on storage object finalize, delete, archive, metadata update
Configuration — firebase.json storage rules path, storageBucket in app config

related.note: Firebase project must be initialized with storage enabled before using Cloud Storage
Security rules reference request.auth for user identity and custom claims
Supabase Storage is an S3-compatible alternative with RLS-based policies and image transforms

scripts.name: Firebase Cloud Storage operations
scripts.description: Upload, download, list, delete, and manage metadata for files in Cloud Storage for Firebase

FIREBASE_API_KEY.description: Firebase API key from project settings

GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS.description: Path to service account JSON for Admin SDK operations

file.description: Local file path for upload or download destination

metadata.description: File metadata including size, contentType, timeCreated

fileList.description: List of file paths at the given prefix

depends.reason: Firebase must be configured with storageBucket before storage operations
